description: Implements 6 key improvements from Cursor's research on scaling long-running autonomous coding: Judge Agent (via Cleo), Continuous Planning, Fresh Start Mechanism, Role-Specific Models, Worker Isolation, and Simplified Integration. These improvements address coordination bottlenecks, agent drift, and enable better scaling of parallel agent execution.

userStories.title: Extend Cleo as Judge Agent
acceptanceCriteria: Add judge_mode flag to Cleo's template configuration
Create new judge.md.hbs partial in templates/agents/cleo/
Implement three decision outputs: continue, complete, fresh_start
Add drift detection logic that identifies risk-averse patterns
Integrate judge evaluation with acceptance-probe.sh.hbs
Add ENABLE_JUDGE_AGENT environment variable to control feature
Judge mode evaluates: acceptance criteria, code quality, task drift, risk aversion
Write status to /workspace/.judge_decision file for workflow consumption
userStories.notes: High impact. Cleo becomes the iteration arbiter, deciding whether work continues, completes, or needs a fresh context start. Based on Cursor's finding that a judge agent at end of each cycle is critical.

userStories.title: Implement Continuous Planning Layer
acceptanceCriteria: Add planner mode to Morgan (templates/agents/morgan/planner.md.hbs)
Planner watches for task completion events via Argo workflow hooks
Planner can create follow-up tasks dynamically in tasks.json
Support spawning sub-planners for complex areas (parallel planning)
Planner wakes up when tasks complete to evaluate next steps
Add continuous_planning flag to cto-config.json defaults.play
Planner re-evaluates task breakdown based on implementation progress
Document planner pattern in templates/skills/context/multi-agent-patterns/SKILL.md
userStories.notes: High impact, high effort. Cursor's breakthrough was planners that continuously explore and create tasks, not just static upfront planning. Morgan shifts from one-shot intake to continuous planning.

userStories.title: Fresh Start Mechanism for Drift Recovery
acceptanceCriteria: Add FRESH_START_THRESHOLD config (default: 3 retries)
Modify retry-loop.sh.hbs to detect when fresh start is needed
When fresh start triggered: CONTINUE_SESSION=false, FRESH_START=true
Fresh start clears accumulated context, starts with only task definition
Add drift_detected signal from Cleo judge evaluation
Log fresh start events for observability
Document fresh start behavior in templates/_shared/partials/retry-loop.sh.hbs
Add freshStartThreshold to cto-config.template.json with documentation
userStories.notes: Medium impact, low effort. Cursor found periodic fresh starts combat drift and tunnel vision. Current retry uses CONTINUE_SESSION=true which preserves problematic context.

userStories.title: Role-Specific Model Configuration
acceptanceCriteria: Add roleModels section to cto-config.template.json
Define model mappings for: planner, worker, judge, reviewer roles
Update crates/config/src/types.rs with RoleModels struct
Planner role defaults to opus (extended thinking)
Worker role defaults to codex (fast, focused execution)
Judge role defaults to opus (reasoning for evaluation)
Reviewer role defaults to sonnet (balance speed/quality)
Workflow template resolves model by role, not just by agent name
Add role_model helper to template rendering context
userStories.notes: Medium impact, low effort. Cursor found different models excel at different roles - GPT-5.2 better planner than codex even though codex is trained for coding.

userStories.title: Enhanced Worker Context Isolation
acceptanceCriteria: Modify coordinator.md.hbs to remove cross-worker awareness
Workers receive ONLY their specific task, no coordination info
Workers have no information about other workers
Workers have no coordination responsibilities
Update subagent templates to enforce isolation
Add worker_isolation flag to subagent dispatch
Document isolation principle in multi-agent-patterns/SKILL.md
Verify workers cannot see or reference peer worker status
userStories.notes: Medium impact, low effort. Cursor's finding: workers don't coordinate with other workers or worry about the big picture. They just grind on their assigned task.

userStories.title: Simplify Atlas to Merge-Only Role
acceptanceCriteria: Reduce Atlas responsibilities to: verify CI, merge PR
Remove quality judgment from Atlas (now handled by Cleo judge)
Remove conflict resolution from Atlas (workers handle themselves)
Atlas only 'blesses' merge after CI passes
Update templates/agents/atlas/ to reflect simplified role
Update play-workflow-template.yaml to skip redundant Atlas checks
Document simplified Atlas role in agent docs
Verify workflow still gates on CI status before merge
userStories.notes: Low-medium impact, low effort. Cursor removed their integrator role - workers handled conflicts themselves. Atlas becomes a simple merge gate, not a quality checkpoint.

successGate.description: All improvements are considered complete when:
conditions: All 6 user stories pass their acceptance criteria
Cleo judge mode can make continue/complete/fresh_start decisions
Morgan planner mode can create follow-up tasks dynamically
Fresh start mechanism triggers after threshold retries
Role-specific models are resolved correctly in workflows
Workers operate in isolated context without peer awareness
Atlas only performs merge verification, not quality judgment
All changes pass cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings -W clippy::pedantic
Documentation is updated to reflect new patterns

steps: 1. Run cargo clippy with pedantic and fix all warnings
2. Run cargo test and fix failing tests
3. Review template syntax for Handlebars errors
4. Verify YAML syntax in workflow templates
5. Check config schema matches types.rs definitions

subagents.@template-expert: Handles Handlebars template modifications. Expert in templates/_shared/ and templates/agents/ structure.
subagents.@rust-expert: Handles crates/ modifications. Expert in config types, controller logic.
subagents.@workflow-expert: Handles Argo workflow YAML modifications. Expert in play-workflow-template.yaml.

keyInsights: Flat coordination with shared files/locks failed - agents became risk-averse
Planners + Workers separation worked - continuous planning, focused workers
Judge agent at end of each cycle determines whether to continue
Periodic fresh starts combat drift and tunnel vision
Different models excel at different roles
Removing complexity (integrator role) often helped more than adding it
Prompts matter more than harness and models
